notes: Intel desktop chipset for LGA775 Pentium 4 / Celeron D (Jun 2004); integrated GMA 900 graphics, DDR2, PCI Express, HD Audio, ICH6 I/O; paired with Grantsdale-P (915P) and Alderwood (925X)
Intel high-end desktop chipset for LGA775 Pentium 4 EE (Jun 2004); no integrated graphics; performance overclocking headroom; partner to Grantsdale
Serial high-speed expansion bus specification; replaced PCI / AGP; 2.5 GT/s Gen1 (2003), 5.0 Gen2 (2007); now at Gen6/Gen7; pervasive from 2005
x16 PCIe slot replacing AGP for PC graphics; introduced with Intel Grantsdale 2004; standard graphics interface since
JEDEC Double Data Rate 2 synchronous DRAM standard; 400-1066 MT/s; 1.8V; mainstream 2005-2008; replaced by DDR3 2007+
Intel codec specification for PC audio; released with Grantsdale 2004; 7.1-channel, 32-bit/192 kHz; replaced AC'97; still used in modern PCs
NetBurst microarchitecture CPU; high clock speeds 1.4-3.8 GHz; last of the Pentium brand before Core 2 Duo (2006)
Major XP service pack Aug 2004; added Windows Security Center, firewall on by default, NX bit / DEP, Bluetooth stack; fundamental XP security overhaul
Microsoft Windows XP Media Center Edition / Vista/7 Media Center; living-room PC for TV, DVR, music; displaced by streaming services and smart TVs
Multi-disk redundancy/performance schemes; levels 0/1/5/6/10; hardware and software implementations; ubiquitous in server storage
